friction_items.description: Phase 9 added scope_tags={domain:[engineering],workflow_shape:[systems_benchmark]} to methodology/method_comparison.yaml. But the router (tool_route) still relies on raw semantic cosine over descriptions and IGNORES scope_tags for ranking. For the prompt 'benchmark 3 sorting algorithms across 5 input sizes with 10 runs each, compare runtime', tool_route returns primary_protocol='methodology/missing_data_strategy' (semantic score 0.6988) because the prompt contains 'compare'. method_comparison only appears in alternatives at 0.6706. For 'run a runtime benchmark of 3 sorting algorithms' the answer is again missing_data_strategy. method_comparison only becomes primary when the prompt literally says 'method comparison algorithms'. The scope_tag mechanism is built but unused by the ranker. A naive AI will load the wrong protocol and waste 1-2 turns realising it.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Inject scope_tags into the semantic ranking: when prompt contains benchmark/runtime/wall-clock/throughput/latency, give a multiplicative boost to protocols whose scope_tags.workflow_shape includes 'systems_benchmark'. Or have tool_route emit a 'scope_warning' when the top-1 semantic match has domain='any' but the prompt has obvious engineering vocabulary.

friction_items.description: Phase 12 was supposed to fill pack completeness gaps but engineering/test/runtime_benchmark.yaml STILL DOES NOT EXIST. The engineering pack ships exactly 3 tools (tool_engineering_fmea_render, tool_engineering_fault_tree_render, tool_engineering_requirements_matrix) and ZERO protocols of its own — protocols/ has only audit/, domain/, guidance/, literature/, methodology/, reproducibility/, synthesis/, visualization/, writing/. There is no engineering/ protocol directory. For an algorithmic/runtime benchmark, the naive AI's ONLY option is still methodology/method_comparison + walk to its engineering addendum, which is step 10 of 11. No tool_runtime_aggregate or tool_stats_paired_test was added either. This was the #1 fix recommended at Phase 15a baseline and it has not landed.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add protocols/engineering/test/runtime_benchmark.yaml with systems-benchmark vocabulary baked in (warm-up, governor, paired Wilcoxon, log-log scaling, language-stdlib baseline) and route it from tool_route triggers. Add tool_engineering_benchmark_aggregate(runs_csv, group_cols, value_col, paired_baseline) and tool_stats_paired_test(metric_a, metric_b, test='wilcoxon|paired_t|mcnemar|delong').

friction_items.description: methodology/method_comparison.yaml step 10 of 11 (engineering_or_systems_benchmark_addendum) still contains the critical guidance (warm-up, governor, Wilcoxon, log-log, stdlib baseline). A naive AI walking the step_summary top-to-bottom locks in ML defaults (split/fold/AUROC) at steps 1-6 BEFORE reaching the addendum. Phase 15a flagged this as needing to fire at step 1 or be split into method_comparison_systems.yaml. Neither happened. The summary's step_summary list still shows the addendum in 10th position with no early-branch detector.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Hoist the addendum: in step 1 add a branch detector 'is the outcome wall-clock/throughput/latency/memory/energy?' and if yes, swap default vocabulary before step 2. Or split protocol into method_comparison_ml.yaml + method_comparison_systems.yaml and have tool_route pick based on prompt vocabulary.

friction_items.description: After the Phase 10 server.py split into modular handlers, sys_tool_describe is broken: handlers/meta_routing.py:278 calls _resolve_tool_name(tool_name) which was not migrated/imported, raising NameError. The dispatcher catches it and returns status='error' with that message. A naive AI trying to read a tool's full description (the recommended path when list_tools shows only short summaries) gets an opaque NameError. This is a real Phase 10 regression that 15b caught.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Import or define _resolve_tool_name inside handlers/meta_routing.py (probably from registry or aliases module). Add a smoke test that exercises sys_tool_describe on every tool name in TOOL_DEFINITIONS so this kind of cross-module reference doesn't slip past the next refactor.

friction_items.description: sys_active_tools(protocol='methodology/method_comparison') returns 13 tools — but NONE of them are execution tools (tool_python_exec, tool_bash_exec, tool_data, tool_synthesize). The shortlist is purely meta (sys_*, tool_route, tool_plan, mem_log). For a protocol whose explicit step 4 is 'Implement each method as a separate sub-task script', omitting tool_python_exec from the active shortlist forces the naive AI to either (a) hunt outside the scope, or (b) skip the executors and pseudo-execute. The shortlist needs the protocol's actual hands-on tools, not just navigation primitives.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Derive active_tools from the protocol's referenced tool list (parse 'tool_python_exec' / 'tool_data' mentions in step bodies + decomposition + expected_outputs path patterns). The router already does this for sub-intent='comparison'; broaden it. Right now active_tools is too lean.

friction_items.description: Phase 15a flagged that paired Wilcoxon / McNemar / DeLong / Nadeau-Bengio have no MCP scaffolding. Phase 9/12/14 did not add tool_stats_paired_test. A naive AI still has to write the statistical test by hand inside tool_python_exec and is precisely where defaults get picked wrong (paired t-test instead of Wilcoxon on heavy-tailed timings). This was one of two named missing-tool gaps from baseline.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add tool_stats_paired_test(metric_a_path, metric_b_path, test='wilcoxon|paired_t|mcnemar|delong|nadeau_bengio') with auto-default selection from data shape + emits methods.md paragraph citing the test.

friction_items.description: The intent_class returned by tool_route for a benchmark prompt is 'methodology'. There is no 'engineering' intent_class even though scope_tags supports domain='engineering'. The classifier hierarchy is L1->L2->L3 still ML-research-oriented. This is why method_comparison loses to missing_data_strategy on semantic distance — both are methodology so the tiebreaker collapses to ML vocabulary similarity.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add 'engineering' as a peer intent_class with sub_intents for runtime_benchmark / system_test / hardware_eval. Route benchmark prompts there and have it cross-link back to method_comparison.

friction_items.description: tool_route.advice always says 'Load <primary> with sys_protocol_get format=summary first (~300 tokens)'. With the new default already being summary, the 'format=summary' arg is now redundant noise in the advice string. A naive AI seeing both the recommended_action='sys_protocol_get(protocol_name=..., format=summary)' and an advice string repeating the same instruction will spend cycles parsing the duplication.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Drop format=summary from advice text since it's the default. Keep recommended_action as the single source of truth; trim advice to one sentence.

confusing_protocols: methodology/method_comparison (systems-benchmark addendum still at step 10 of 11)
methodology/evaluation_design (still ML-vocabulary only; no systems-benchmark mode)
methodology/missing_data_strategy (router consistently picks this for benchmark prompts via semantic noise — a real failure mode)
improvements_vs_baseline: Tool surface dropped from 222 unique names (Phase 15a) to 146 (-34%). Major reduction in cognitive load.
tool_audit_* family collapsed from 25+ entries to 2 (tool_audit_findings, tool_audit_quality_full). Phase 15a's #1 recommendation landed.
All tools now carry status:'live' + pack:'core|<pack>' — Phase 15a's deprecation-stub pollution is gone; no 'tool_figure_create was removed' entries showing as tools.

MCP-level 'instructions' field now ships at handshake naming the boot sequence (sys_boot -> tool_route -> sys_protocol_get summary -> sys_active_tools). A naive AI no longer has to guess the entry point.

sys_protocol_get default format is now 'summary' (was 'full'). Loading method_comparison returns step_summary + quality_bar in ~2.5KB instead of the full 3K-token body. Phase 15a recommendation #7 landed.

Protocols now have scope_tags={domain, audience, workflow_shape} (method_comparison correctly tagged engineering+systems_benchmark, missing_data_strategy correctly tagged any+experiment_pipeline). The metadata is in place even though the router doesn't yet rank with it.

tool_route output now includes recommended_action (e.g. 'sys_protocol_get(protocol_name=..., format=summary)') alongside the why_matched field from Phase 11a. The recommended_action makes the next step explicit.
sys_active_tools returns a tight 13-tool shortlist for method_comparison instead of the naive AI having to triage 200+ tools manually.
server.py monolith (7499 lines) split into a modular server/ package with per-category handler modules + per-category tool_definitions modules. Maintainable now; navigable by an AI reading the code.
Engineering/qualitative/humanities/wet_lab/theory_math packs each ship as named packs with explicit pack=<name> annotation, so a naive AI listing tools can see which are core vs pack-gated.

tool_synthesize 'just works' end-to-end on a fresh workspace — produced synthesis/paper.md + references.bib without requiring the AI to chain 5 separate synthesis_* tools (the synthesis_* family was consolidated).
remaining_recommendations: v2.1.0: Make tool_route ACTUALLY USE scope_tags. Currently the metadata is on every protocol but the ranker still uses raw semantic cosine over descriptions. Benchmark prompts route to missing_data_strategy instead of method_comparison. This is the single biggest remaining friction for naive AIs.
v2.1.0: Add protocols/engineering/test/runtime_benchmark.yaml as a first-class protocol — the engineering pack still has zero protocols, only 3 renderers. Phase 12 left this gap.

v2.1.0: Hoist methodology/method_comparison's engineering-systems-benchmark addendum from step 10 of 11 to step 1 (branch detector), or split into method_comparison_ml.yaml + method_comparison_systems.yaml. A naive AI still walks 9 ML-flavoured steps before learning warm-up + Wilcoxon + log-log scaling exist.
v2.1.0: Fix the Phase 10 regression — sys_tool_describe raises NameError on _resolve_tool_name. Add a smoke test that calls sys_tool_describe on every tool in TOOL_DEFINITIONS.

v2.1.0: Add tool_stats_paired_test(test='wilcoxon|paired_t|mcnemar|delong') and tool_engineering_benchmark_aggregate(runs_csv, group_cols, value_col, paired_baseline). The naive AI still hand-writes statistics inside tool_python_exec and the wrong default (paired t over Wilcoxon) is exactly where benchmark conclusions go wrong.

v2.1.0: Broaden sys_active_tools to include each protocol's actually-referenced executors (tool_python_exec, tool_bash_exec, tool_data, tool_synthesize) — the current 13-tool list for method_comparison omits all execution tools, forcing the AI back to global triage.
v2.1.0: Drop the redundant 'format=summary' from tool_route.advice now that summary is the default; recommended_action is the single source of truth.
v2.1.0: Add an 'engineering' intent_class peer to 'methodology' so the L1->L2->L3 router can distinguish a runtime benchmark from a missing-data analysis without a semantic-cosine coin flip.
